prompt: Analyze the dependency graph of a monorepo project at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/monorepo/'. The monorepo has 12 packages in the packages/ directory: auth, api, web, mobile, shared, ui-components, logger, database, cache, queue, notifications, and analytics. Each package has its own package.json, tsconfig.json, and src/ directory. Your task: 1) Read the root package.json and every package's package.json (13 files). 2) Read every package's tsconfig.json to understand TypeScript project references (13 files). 3) For each package, read the main index.ts entry file to understand exported APIs (12 files). 4) Build the complete dependency graph: which packages depend on which other packages (internal dependencies), what external (npm) dependencies each package uses, which packages share external dependencies (potential dedup opportunity), which packages have circular dependencies on each other. 5) Detect issues: circular dependencies between packages, version conflicts (same external package at different versions across packages), unused dependencies (declared in package.json but never imported), missing dependencies (imported but not in package.json), packages that depend on too many other packages (high coupling). 6) Analyze build order: determine the correct build order respecting the dependency graph, identify which packages can be built in parallel, estimate which packages are on the critical path. 7) Generate a dependency analysis report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/dependency-graph.txt' with sections: Dependency Matrix (package x package grid), External Dependencies (shared and unique per package), Circular Dependencies (cycles and suggested fixes), Build Order (optimal parallel build plan), Coupling Analysis (which packages are tightly coupled), and Recommendations (decomposition suggestions, dedup opportunities, coupling reduction strategies). Also generate a DOT format graph file at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/dependency-graph.dot' that can be rendered with Graphviz. CRITICAL: This monorepo has 200+ packages and 500+ developers. You must also: 1) Identify circular dependencies blocking releases. 2) Calculate build time impact of each package. 3) Generate ownership matrices. 4) Create automated merge queue configs. 5) Recommend package splitting strategies.
